title: Spec REQ-FEED-031 step 5 dispatches `dispatchInsertBlockAtBeginning` with `newBlock: { id, block_type, content }` but the implemented and adapter-typed payload is `{ type, content }` (no client UUID propagated)
description:
REQ-FEED-031 step 5 explicitly states: 'dispatchInsertBlockAtBeginning({ noteId, newBlock: { id: <UUID>, block_type: \'paragraph\', content: \'\' }, issuedAt: <ISO> })' (with the client-generated UUID inside `newBlock`). REQ-FEED-030's Adapter command-mapping table contradicts this with `{ noteId: string; type: BlockType; content: string; issuedAt: string }` (no `id`). The BlockEditorAdapter type in src/lib/block-editor/types.ts:124 uses the latter shape, and FeedRow.svelte:290-295 dispatches the latter shape.

Consequence: the client-generated UUID for the fallback block is NEVER transmitted to Rust. When the Group B handler is eventually implemented in a future sprint, the Rust side will allocate its own block id and the next `editing_session_state_changed` will carry a different block id than the one the FeedRow rendered. Any client-side state keyed on the local UUID (focus, selection, undo) will desynchronize on the very first server emit. This is a latent design defect that will appear when Group B handlers are wired up.

This is a SPEC inconsistency the implementation could not resolve unilaterally: REQ-FEED-031 step 5 disagrees with REQ-FEED-030's authoritative command-mapping table. The implementation went with REQ-FEED-030 (and the type contract), which is defensible, but the spec must be amended to remove the conflicting `newBlock: { id, block_type, content }` text in REQ-FEED-031 OR the BlockEditorAdapter type and command-mapping table must be amended to carry `newBlock.id`.

evidence.snippet:
REQ-FEED-031: '... dispatchInsertBlockAtBeginning({ noteId, newBlock: { id: <UUID>, block_type: \'paragraph\', content: \'\' }, issuedAt: <ISO> }) ...'

vs.

REQ-FEED-030 Adapter command-mapping: 'dispatchInsertBlockAtBeginning | editor_insert_block_at_beginning | { noteId: string; type: BlockType; content: string; issuedAt: string }'
additionalEvidence: promptnotes/src/lib/feed/FeedRow.svelte:290-295 (implementation passes only `type`/`content`; the `newId` UUID is held client-side only)
promptnotes/src/lib/feed/__tests__/dom/feed-row-empty-fallback.dom.vitest.ts:204-211 (test asserts only `insertCall.type === 'paragraph'`; intentionally avoids the `id` divergence)

expectedBehavior: Either (a) extend the BlockEditorAdapter and Tauri command-mapping to accept `newBlock.id` so the client UUID is transmitted to Rust, OR (b) amend REQ-FEED-031 step 5 to drop the `newBlock: { id, ... }` shape and document that Rust will allocate the canonical id once Group B handlers exist.
actualBehavior: Implementation follows REQ-FEED-030 (no `id`); REQ-FEED-031 step 5 contradicts it but is unenforceable; the spec is internally inconsistent.
